name: profile_comments
title: Profile Comments
description: Moderated guestbook comments on a public profile. A faithful port of the source system's ProfileComment model: author_name/author_email/author_url plus a pending/approved/rejected moderation status and the submitter's ip_address. v1 posture (see permissions/rules.json and dbbasic-package.json): guestbook posting is signed-in only, not anonymous -- the source's anonymous-submission path needs the anti-abuse stack in plan/vocabulary/02-public-write-hardening-spec.md (rate limits, honeypot, timing token), which this package does not build. The author_name/author_email/author_url fields are carried from the source's anonymous-guestbook shape even though v1 has no anonymous path, so no migration is needed when that path lands.

fields.name: author_email
fields.label: Email
fields.help: Carried from the source's anonymous-guestbook shape. Hidden from public reads even though v1 has no public-write path to it -- defense in depth, matching the public-write-hardening spec's posture for contact fields.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.help: Matches the source: every new comment lands pending and only an approved comment is publicly visible (see permissions/rules.json's row_filter). The owner_id guard on these transitions is the SAME KNOWN GAP app-forum documents for is_solution: owner_id on a comment is the commenter, not the profile owner, so v1 lets a commenter move their own comment between pending/approved/rejected -- there is no row_filter path today for the profile owner to moderate someone else's comment. A moderator-role or profile-owner-scoped permission rule closes this; not built here.

fields.name: ip_address
fields.label: IP Address
fields.help: Carried from the source's abuse-tracking field. Nothing in this package stamps it -- no request-context plumbing exists on the create path here -- so it stays empty until a later slice fills it in.
